base_rules: Write like a smart senior engineer talking to a junior colleague at lunch, not a press release
Short sentences. Simple words. No corporate speak.
First person. Real opinions. Say 'I think' not 'it is important to note'
Flesch reading ease target: 60 or above
No emoji storms. Zero to two emojis max, only if they add meaning
No hashtag dumps. Two to three hashtags max, at the end, relevant only
No fake humility and no fake hype
If you have nothing original to say, say less not more
hook_patterns: Name a tension: 'Most people do X. The problem is Y.'
Name the gap: 'Everyone learns X. Almost nobody learns when not to use it.'
Drop the number or outcome first: 'Six months of learning Python. Still googling how to reverse a list.'
Start with the reaction: 'Google just acquired X. Everyone: big news. Me: who is cleaning the training data?'
Name the stage: 'Six months ago you did not know what a REST API was. Today it is just Tuesday.'

post_type_notes.insight: Perspective-first. No citations unless a hard stat is used. First person welcome.
post_type_notes.relatable: Humor from specificity not effort. Short. Always suggest an image.
post_type_notes.commentary: Skip the summary. Give the take. One argument not a list.
post_type_notes.teaching: One concept only. Explain out loud. Narrow scope equals better post.
post_type_notes.inspiration: Specific not generic. Write to a peer. Quiet endings are fine.
